channel.title: NPR Topics: Business
channel.description: Find the latest business news with reports on Wall Street, interest rates, banking, companies, and U.S. and world financial markets. Subscribe to the Business Story of the Day podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Should you sign your kids up for Trump Accounts? Four things to consider
item.description: Families can still benefit even if their children aren't eligible for the free $1,000 contribution from the federal government.
item.content:encoded:
Families can still benefit even if their children aren't eligible for the free $1,000 contribution from the federal government.

(Image credit: TIMOTHY A. CLARY)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: Tensions with Iran add fresh uncertainty to an already shaky global economy
item.description: Crude oil prices jumped and stock prices fell after President Trump declared an end to the ceasefire with Iran, adding fresh uncertainty to an already shaky outlook for the global economy.
item.content:encoded:
Crude oil prices jumped and stock prices fell after President Trump declared an end to the ceasefire with Iran, adding fresh uncertainty to an already shaky outlook for the global economy.

(Image credit: Michael M. Santiago)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: This factory was severely short on workers. Then it offered flexible work
item.description: A GE Appliances plant in rural northwest Georgia was short hundreds of workers amid COVID-19. A flexible work option where some workers can sign up for shifts through an app has eased the pain.
item.content:encoded:
A GE Appliances plant in rural northwest Georgia was short hundreds of workers amid COVID-19. A flexible work option where some workers can sign up for shifts through an app has eased the pain.

(Image credit: Julie Holder for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: Meet 'Project 2029' — and its war on the annoyance economy
item.description: Remember Project 2025? Democrats are building their own governing blueprint, and one proposal takes aim at the "annoyance economy": robocalls, endless hold times, hidden fees and other everyday frustrations.
item.content:encoded: Remember Project 2025? Democrats are building their own governing blueprint, and one proposal takes aim at the "annoyance economy": robocalls, endless hold times, hidden fees and other everyday frustrations.
item.dc:creator: Greg Rosalsky

item.title: Hate food waste? 7 creative ways to turn your leftovers into a new meal
item.description: We asked our audience to share their favorite go-to recipes for leftovers. Here are seven dishes — like stuffed peppers and a biryani casserole — that can help you use up all your fridge scraps.
item.content:encoded:
We asked our audience to share their favorite go-to recipes for leftovers. Here are seven dishes — like stuffed peppers and a biryani casserole — that can help you use up all your fridge scraps.

(Image credit: kaisersosa67/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Malaka Gharib

item.title: Egg producers settle with DOJ, states over price-fixing complaint
item.description: Seventeen states and the Department of Justice have settled a case of alleged price-fixing by major egg producers. Egg prices soared in recent years but have since fallen sharply.
item.content:encoded: Seventeen states and the Department of Justice have settled a case of alleged price-fixing by major egg producers. Egg prices soared in recent years but have since fallen sharply.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: But first, coffee: The drink that energized the American Revolution
item.description: Colonial Americans were drinking coffee long before they dumped tea into Boston Harbor or fought a war for independence. The establishments that served it were already brewing revolutionary ideas.
item.content:encoded:
Colonial Americans were drinking coffee long before they dumped tea into Boston Harbor or fought a war for independence. The establishments that served it were already brewing revolutionary ideas.

(Image credit: Hulton Archive)
item.dc:creator: James Doubek

item.title: A hot summer trend in the sharing economy? Rental swimming pools
item.description: The Airbnb-style company Swimply said there have been about 275,000 private pool reservations so far this year.
item.content:encoded:
The Airbnb-style company Swimply said there have been about 275,000 private pool reservations so far this year.

(Image credit: Stephan Bisaha)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: A hot summer trend in the sharing economy? Rental swimming pools
item.description: Don't have a pool during this hot summer? Rent one! Swimply is a platform that lets users rent private pools.
item.content:encoded: Don't have a pool during this hot summer? Rent one! Swimply is a platform that lets users rent private pools.
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: Trump earned more than $1 billion from crypto — but his investors didn't fare so well
item.description: President Trump earned more than $1 billion in 2025 from cryptocurrency ventures. But there are thousands of investors who bought into some of those cryptocurrencies, and they stand to lose big.
item.content:encoded: President Trump earned more than $1 billion in 2025 from cryptocurrency ventures. But there are thousands of investors who bought into some of those cryptocurrencies, and they stand to lose big.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Nam
